seoTitle: How to Add People to a Jira Project
metaDescription: Learn how to invite teammates to a Jira project and assign them the right role so they can start collaborating on issues.
intro: Getting a new teammate set up in Jira means adding them to the right project with the appropriate access level. This guide covers inviting people to a project and assigning them a role.

steps.heading: Open your project settings
steps.body: Navigate to the people or access settings for the specific project you want to add someone to.

steps.heading: Search for the person
steps.body: Look up the teammate by name or email, whether they're already part of your organization's Jira instance or need an invite.

steps.heading: Assign a project role
steps.body: Choose the appropriate role for the person, which determines what they can view and do within the project.

steps.heading: Confirm and send the invite
steps.body: Add them to the project, which for new users may trigger an invitation email to join.

steps.heading: Verify their access
steps.body: Check that the new team member can see the project and has the level of access intended for their role.

faq.question: What roles are typically available when adding someone to a Jira project?
faq.answer: Common roles include administrator, member, and viewer, though exact naming and permissions can vary depending on your project's configuration.

faq.question: Can I add someone who isn't part of my organization yet?
faq.answer: Yes, if they don't already have an account, adding them typically triggers an invitation for them to join your Jira instance.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to add people to a project?
faq.answer: Yes, adding users to a project generally requires administrative access at the project or site level.

faq.question: Can I remove someone from a project later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can revisit the project's people settings at any time to remove access or change someone's role.
